practical_recommendation: Use roughly 5-30 reps for hypertrophy when effort and technique are appropriate; default many compound lifts to 5-12 and stable isolations to 8-25.
important_exception_or_limitation: Very light loads need closer proximity to failure; pain, cardio, grip, or balance can make a nominal range unproductive.

practical_recommendation: Start near 6-10 fractional hard sets per muscle weekly and adjust; many trained males may use 10-20, but returns diminish and muscle-specific thresholds are not established.
important_exception_or_limitation: Ranges are operational and depend on effort, exercise, indirect work, session distribution, calories, sleep, and individual response.

practical_recommendation: Choose 1-3 weekly exposures per muscle to distribute volume; twice weekly is a convenient default, not a hypertrophy requirement.
important_exception_or_limitation: Very high per-session volume may justify higher frequency; low-volume programs can work at once weekly.

practical_recommendation: Keep most productive sets around 0-4 RIR, generally closer for light-load/high-rep work and farther for high-skill compounds.
important_exception_or_limitation: RIR reporting is imprecise and failure is not required; the exact optimal RIR is unresolved.

practical_recommendation: Rest long enough to preserve output: often 2-5 minutes for demanding compounds and 1-3 minutes for stable isolations.
important_exception_or_limitation: Hypertrophy differences beyond about 90 seconds are small and uncertain; time constraints and supersets change the tradeoff.

practical_recommendation: Count load, reps, lower RPE/higher RIR, better ROM, and better execution as progression only under comparable conditions and multi-session confirmation.
important_exception_or_limitation: Deterministic thresholds are operational inferences; no universal plateau or regression cut point is validated.

practical_recommendation: Choose machines or free weights by target-muscle loading, comfort, skill, stability, and progression resolution; neither is inherently superior for hypertrophy.
important_exception_or_limitation: Strength transfer remains modality- and task-specific.

practical_recommendation: Use the largest pain-free controlled ROM and include long-muscle-length loading where practical.
important_exception_or_limitation: Long-length advantages are small/heterogeneous and muscle-specific; shortened partials can still be useful.

practical_recommendation: Target about 1.6-2.2 g/kg/day; in lean deficits consider about 1.8-2.4 g/kg body weight or 2.3-3.1 g/kg fat-free mass.
important_exception_or_limitation: Higher intake is not automatically better and medical protein restrictions require clinician guidance.

practical_recommendation: Use a moderate deficit and slower loss when leaner or more advanced; preserve load exposure and reduce only low-value volume when recovery deteriorates.
important_exception_or_limitation: The approximately 500 kcal/day inflection is an average meta-regression estimate, not a universal safe limit.

practical_recommendation: Recomposition is most plausible in untrained, detrained, novice, higher-body-fat, or previously under-proteined males; advanced lean males should expect little.
important_exception_or_limitation: Short-term DXA lean-mass changes include water/glycogen and do not equal contractile tissue.

practical_recommendation: Use the smallest surplus that supports a credible gain trend: roughly 0.25-0.5% body weight/week for novices, 0.15-0.3% for intermediates, and 0.1-0.25% for advanced males.
important_exception_or_limitation: These are practical review-based rates; trials do not establish an optimal surplus or universal muscle-to-fat ratio.

practical_recommendation: A large volume reduction can often maintain muscle if meaningful load/effort exposure remains; start around 25-50% of recent productive volume and monitor.
important_exception_or_limitation: Direct evidence in currently trained young males and during calorie restriction is sparse.

practical_recommendation: Use drop sets, rest-pause, and similar methods mainly for time efficiency or preference, not because they are proven superior.
important_exception_or_limitation: Method-specific evidence varies and fatigue can change set comparability.
